patches.find: The dome provides no derived prediction. The dome's monitor.py hardcodes a prospective 2026 eclipse prediction (ΔB = −18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 ≈ −29 nT) but shows no derivation from dome geometry, aetheric medium properties, or firmament conductivity. The dome model observes that eclipses produce ~10 nT variations — known since the 1930s — and relabels the cause as 'aetheric.'
patches.replace: The dome's scaling has geometric content, but not at the magnitude level. The dome publishes a scaling equation δZstation = baseline × eclipse-coverage × FSF, where FSF = H(r)/r is a station-to-station factor derived from dome geometry (firmament height H divided by the station's dome-coordinate radius r). At the cluster level this ratio reproduces the observed 9-station pattern to Pearson r ≈ 0.9999 — we grant it as a genuine geometric derivation (verified independently in the WIN-010/WIN-068 cluster). What the dome does not derive is the absolute magnitude: the 'baseline' term is imported from the 2017 Boulder single-station observation, not from any dome parameter. The dome's monitor.py hardcodes a prospective 2026 eclipse prediction (ΔB = −18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 ≈ −29 nT) on top of this imported baseline but derives no magnitude from firmament height, aetheric medium properties, or firmament conductivity. The dome model observes that eclipses produce ~10 nT variations — known since the 1930s — relabels the cause as 'aetheric,' and scales the station-to-station pattern with a geometric ratio while importing the absolute magnitude from observation.
patches.rationale: EXP-235: WIN-025 detail_evidence P4 FSF setup. Curmudgeon c3 H3 (moderate) flagged evidence/verdict disjoint after EXP-229 added FSF concession to verdict. This patch adds FSF = H(r)/r setup to evidence so the verdict retraction has an evidence-level premise. Closes ISS-1463.

patches.find: Even granting that FSF = H(r)/r is a valid geometric scaling, Section 1.8 (The Coordinate Collapse) shows the disc-radius values that scaling feeds on are themselves computed from observed Polaris elevation angles, travelling through the same n(r) medium the dome concedes is 300–6,000× more refractive than Earth's atmosphere. Every dome-coordinate r in the FSF table inherits that contamination, so even the one genuine derivation the dome has produced anchors on numbers that are not what the dome thinks they are.
patches.replace: For the Section 1.8 Coordinate Collapse critique of the dome-coordinate r values that feed FSF, see WIN-010's verdict, where FSF actually enters the predicted signal and inherits that refractive contamination. It is out of scope here: the 39-eclipse ensemble evaluated in WIN-068 does not invoke FSF at all.
patches.rationale: EXP-236: WIN-068 detail_verdict_text P5 scope leak. P5 attacked FSF via Section 1.8 Coordinate Collapse inside a WIN whose own scope says FSF is a spectator for the 39-eclipse ensemble. Relocates critique to WIN-010 where FSF actually enters the prediction. WIN-010 already carries the Coordinate Collapse paragraph. Closes ISS-1466.

patches.find: Schumann resonance does respond to geomagnetic storms, but published direction is mixed — Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 and Pazos et al. 2019 both report amplitude enhancement during storm conditions; other papers report suppression under different conditions. The dome claims ≥30% suppression but provides no equation that would select suppression over enhancement, and its cavity formula predicts a ~22 Hz baseline rather than the observed 7.83 Hz.
patches.replace: Schumann resonance does respond to geomagnetic storms, but published direction is mixed. Pazos et al. 2019 reports amplitude enhancement during moderate-to-severe storms (Dst < −70 nT); Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 reports cavity-Q enhancement during solar proton events (a related but distinct driver class); other studies report suppression under different conditions. The dome claims ≥30% suppression but provides no equation that would select suppression over enhancement, and its cavity formula predicts a ~22 Hz baseline rather than the observed 7.83 Hz.
patches.rationale: WIN-061 c5 H1 (major): tldr_evidence lumped S&F and Pazos together as both reporting amplitude enhancement. S&F actually reports cavity-Q enhancement (not amplitude) during SPEs. Revised TLDR preserves Pazos-primary / S&F-supporting structure from detail_evidence and uses correct metric for S&F. New issue ISS-1475, self-apply and close.

patches.find: G3+ storms cause ≥30% Schumann amplitude suppression within 6 hours.
patches.replace: G3+ storms cause ≥30% Schumann amplitude suppression within 6 hours. PRED-SR-SUPPRESS was registered 2026-03-23, 1–3 days after the March 20–22 G3 geomagnetic storm (NOAA SWPC confirmed, Kp reached 7); near-real-time SR amplitude data was available during the storm (see predictions.json entry PRED-SR-SUPPRESS detail_reasoning). A companion prediction PRED-093 with cryptographic hash was registered 2026-04-04, 13 days after the same storm. A prediction and its cryptographically-timestamped duplicate, both registered after the only triggering event, cannot establish prospective status no matter the hash.
patches.rationale: WIN-061 c3 H5 / c4 H3 / c5 H2 (major carry-forward): ISS-1418 PRED-SR-SUPPRESS timestamp-forward registration was never inserted into the WIN text despite being tracked across three review cycles. This self-apply inserts the two timestamp-forward sentences near the triggering sentence. Closes ISS-1418.

WIN-010 c8 H1 (moderate): pre-registered comparator in P7 cites Y-component response envelope but dome predicts delta_Z. Two-word swap to match the dome prediction component. New issue ISS-1476, self-apply and close.

patches.find: FSF = H(r)/r was introduced specifically to repair that failure.
patches.replace: the eclipse-specific δZₛₜₐₜᵢₒₙ = baseline × coverage × FSF formula applying H(r)/r to eclipse scaling was introduced at v45 specifically to repair that failure, against the same 9-station dataset.
patches.rationale: WIN-025 c4 H4 (moderate): overstated claim that FSF was introduced specifically to repair the failure. The evolution log records the v31 failure and v45 FSF introduction, but does not use the word "specifically". Rewrites to name the formula and version for precision. New issue ISS-1477.
